fault_hypothesis: The condition `if not clamp` on line 65 is inverted: clamping logic runs when clamp=False and is skipped when clamp=True, exactly the opposite of the intended behavior.

constraints.intent: When clamp=True the clamping branch should be entered, but `not clamp` means it is skipped — this expr is always False, flagging the inverted condition.
After the `if not clamp` branch body executes, clamp must indeed be False; if clamp=True caused entry here the condition is violated.
After the else branch (no clamping applied), clamp must be True; if clamp=False caused entry here the logic is inverted.

spec.expr: not clamp or (min(y0, y1) <= __cbfl_result <= max(y0, y1))

constraints.intent: When clamp=True the returned value must lie within [min(y0,y1), max(y0,y1)]; failing tests show extrapolated values are returned instead.
